h1: Cockroach Extermination in NYC — Eliminated for Good
heroParagraph: Cockroaches are the most common pest in New York City — and the hardest to eliminate without professional treatment. A single German cockroach egg case contains 48 eggs with a 28-day incubation period, meaning a small problem becomes a massive infestation within weeks. Our licensed exterminators use gel bait, IGR treatments, and crack-and-crevice applications to eliminate cockroach populations at the colony level — not just the ones you can see.
bodyParagraphs: NYC has two cockroach species that matter: German cockroaches and American cockroaches. German cockroaches are the small, light-brown ones concentrated in kitchens and bathrooms — the ones you see scattering when you flip on the light. American cockroaches, often called water bugs, are larger, reddish-brown, and typically found in basements, boiler rooms, and sewer connections. Both require professional treatment. Both will outlast any store-bought spray you throw at them.

The reason over-the-counter products fail is chemistry. Retail sprays are repellents — cockroaches smell them and avoid the area, retreating deeper into wall voids, shared plumbing chases, and neighboring apartments. Professional gel bait is the opposite: non-repellent, irresistible to cockroaches, and designed to spread through the colony as cockroaches consume dead colony members. One treatment reaches hundreds of cockroaches our applicators never directly touched.
signs: Live cockroaches visible during daytime hours — overcrowding is forcing them out of hiding
Egg cases (oothecae) — small brown capsules about 1/3 inch long, found in dark areas
Droppings — black specks resembling ground pepper near food sources and appliances
Musty, oily odor — large infestations produce a distinctive smell
Smear marks — brown streaks along walls where cockroaches travel
Shed skins — cockroaches molt multiple times before reaching adulthood
treatmentApproach: Every cockroach job starts with a full inspection to identify species, locate nesting areas, and assess the extent of the infestation. Treatment typically includes professional-grade gel bait placed in precise locations inside cabinets, behind appliances, and in wall voids; IGR (insect growth regulator) treatments that sterilize immature cockroaches; and dust formulations applied inside electrical outlets and voids where liquid products can't safely reach. For severe multi-unit building infestations, we coordinate with property management for building-wide treatment to prevent cockroaches from migrating between units.

faqs.q: How long does cockroach treatment take to work?
faqs.a: Gel bait treatments begin working within 24–72 hours. You'll notice a significant reduction in cockroach activity within the first week as the bait spreads through the colony. Complete elimination typically takes 2–4 weeks depending on the severity of the infestation. We schedule a follow-up inspection to confirm results.

faqs.q: Do I need to leave my apartment during treatment?
faqs.a: For most cockroach treatments using gel bait and dust formulations, you don't need to vacate. We'll ask you to stay out of treated areas for 30–60 minutes while products are applied. If we use spray treatments in specific areas, we may ask for 2–4 hours. We'll give you specific instructions before we start.

faqs.q: Why do I keep seeing cockroaches after treatment?
faqs.a: This is normal and actually a good sign — it means cockroaches are coming out to feed on the bait. Activity typically increases briefly after treatment before dropping sharply. If you're still seeing significant activity after two weeks, contact us for a follow-up visit included in your guarantee.

faqs.q: My neighbor has cockroaches — will I get them too?
faqs.a: In multi-unit buildings, yes — cockroaches travel freely through shared walls, plumbing, and electrical conduits. The most effective solution is building-wide treatment coordinated with your property manager. We handle building-wide programs regularly and can contact your management directly if needed.

faqs.q: Are your cockroach treatments safe for my baby / elderly parent / pet?
faqs.a: Yes. Professional gel bait is placed in precise locations inside voids and crevices, not on open surfaces. IGR treatments are applied similarly. Exposure risk for people and pets is minimal. We'll walk you through specific precautions for your household before starting.

faqs.q: Can cockroaches come back after professional treatment?
faqs.a: They can — particularly in multi-unit buildings where cockroaches can migrate from untreated adjacent units. This is why our treatments include a 30-day guarantee and why we recommend ongoing quarterly maintenance for building-wide prevention. Single-family homes and isolated units maintain elimination longer.

faqs.q: How much does cockroach extermination cost in NYC?
faqs.a: Cockroach treatment starts at $150–$400 for a standard apartment. Severe infestations, larger properties, or multi-unit building programs are quoted after inspection. We provide a written estimate before any work begins. No hidden fees, no surprises.

faqs.q: What can I do to prevent cockroaches from coming back?
faqs.a: Fix leaky faucets immediately — cockroaches can survive weeks without food but only days without water. Store food in sealed airtight containers. Don't leave pet food out overnight. Seal gaps around pipes and electrical outlets with caulk. Take out kitchen trash daily. And if you live in a multi-unit building, address the problem building-wide.
